EnzymeName: aquacobalamin reductase. This entry has been deleted since no specific enzyme catalysing this activity has been identified and it has been shown that aquacobalamin is efficiently reduced by free dihydroflavins and by non-specific reduced flavoproteins.
cob(II)yrinic acid a,c-diamide reductase. This activity is now known to be catalyzed by EC 2.5.1.17, corrinoid adenosyltransferase
bile-acid 7alpha-dehydroxylase. Now known to be catalyzed by multiple enzymes.
succinate dehydrogenase. The activity is included in EC 1.3.5.1, succinate dehydrogenase (quinone).
NADH dehydrogenase (quinone). Identical to EC 1.6.5.9, NADH:quinone reductase (non-electrogenic)
The activity is now known to be catalysed by EC 1.7.2.3, trimethylamine-N-oxide reductase.
NADH dehydrogenase. The activity is covered by EC 7.1.1.2, NADH:ubiquinone reductase (H+-translocating)

nitrate reductase (NADH), EC 1.7.1.2, nitrate reductase [NAD(P)H], EC 1.7.1.3, nitrate reductase (NADPH), EC 1.7.5.1, nitrate reductase (quinone), EC 1.7.7.2, nitrate reductase (ferredoxin) and EC 1.9.6.1, nitrate reductase (cytochrome)
hydrogensulfite reductase, now known to be an in vitro artifact of EC 1.8.99.5, dissimilatory sulfite reductase

phosphatidylinositol alpha-mannosyltransferase. Newer studies have shown that this is catalysed by two independent activities now covered by EC 2.4.1.345, phosphatidyl-myo-inositol alpha-mannosyl transferase and EC 2.4.1.346, phosphatidyl-myo-inositol dimannoside synthase
Enterobacter ribonuclease, since the primary reaction is that of a lyase
bisphosphoglycerate phosphatase. Recent studies have shown that this is a partial activity of EC 5.4.2.11, phosphoglycerate mutase (2,3-diphosphoglycerate-dependent)
nucleotidase. The activity may be that of an acid phosphatase.
Xaa-His dipeptidase. The activity is covered by EC 3.4.13.18, cytosol nonspecific dipeptidase and EC 3.4.13.20, beta-Ala-His dipeptidase.
adenosinetriphosphatase. Enzymes previously listed under this number are now listed separately under EC 5.6 and EC 7.
DNA 5'-3' helicase and EC 5.6.2.4, DNA 3'-5' helicase
UDP-glucuronate 5-epimerase. The enzyme has never been purified and the activity was later shown not to exist.
aminodeoxychorismate synthase. As ATP is not hydrolysed during the reaction, the classification of the enzyme as a ligase was incorrect
